---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 3d54116abd90b77f3bdc84949689746a4a28f41c
findings:
  - { id: judged-or-regen-masks-unwalkable-history, kind: judged, text: "fetchAncestorBundle (cmd/verdi/sync_ancestor.go:82-85) deliberately wraps an ancestry-enumeration failure (gitx.Log error after a commit-itself miss) in forge.ErrNoBundle. In the no---or-regen path this is disclosed, but under --or-regen runSync's `errors.Is(err, forge.ErrNoBundle) \u0026\u0026 orRegen` branch (cmd/verdi/sync.go:228) matches it and silently regenerates locally without printing the wrapped error — so a history that could not be walked at all is treated as evidence of absence, and a bundle that may exist at a real ancestor is bypassed with no disclosure that the walk never ran. dc-1's posture is that only a genuine no-bundle result is a fallback signal and non-absence failures surface through the operational path; an unenumerable history is closer to the latter. Practical reachability is low (resolveRefCommit already ran git rev-parse in the same root), which tempers severity. (confidence 0.35)" }
  - { id: judged-shallow-clone-silently-bounds-unbounded-walk, kind: judged, text: "ac-2/dc-1 promise the walk is unbounded — 'continues to the ref's root' — and the exhausted refusal names 'the ref's entire history'. In a shallow or partial clone (fetch-depth:1 is the common CI checkout), `git log \u003ccommit\u003e` (gitx.Log, sync_ancestor.go:34) silently stops at the shallow boundary, so the refusal at sync_ancestor.go:109 claims 'anywhere in N commit(s) walked (oldest..commit)' when only the truncated local graph was walked, and a bundle at a deeper true ancestor is refused with no disclosure that the history itself was incomplete. The fold's gitx.IsAncestor shares the same local-graph blindness, so the two surfaces cannot disagree (the dc-1 authority clause holds), but the spec's 'exhausting the ref's entire history' is silently narrowed to 'exhausting the clone's available history' with no shallow-repo detection or disclosure. (confidence 0.25)" }
  - { id: judged-env-wins-register-shortfall, kind: judged, text: "AC-1's behavioral register asks for an integration test 'proving the explicit CI env still wins byte-identically when both it and a resolvable origin are present' using 'fixturegit plus internal/forge/fake'. The delivered TestGithubIdentifier_CIEnvWinsOverResolvableOrigin (cmd/verdi/sync_test.go:946) proves env-wins at the githubOwnerRepo seam with a real fixturegit remote read via gitx.RemoteURL, but never drives runSync/cmdSync and uses no fake forge — no test observes a sync run end-to-end addressing the forge with the env-resolved identifier while a conflicting origin is configured. This is a defensible register substitution (identifier resolution structurally happens in cmdSync before deps exist, and cmdSync cannot take an injected forge), but it is a quieter register than the spec's text names, undisclosed. (confidence 0.20)" }
digest: sha256:a52cc580f67831100b5d7a8195c059036367b4eb4f7c6d45c4ab2e7e4851f8cd
integrity: sha256:c1abe32fa7e02935797c394473a2829b1a6f8ac8b2987bf5c34aa259deca6c1a
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"or-regen-masks-unwalkable-history\",\"text\":\"fetchAncestorBundle (cmd/verdi/sync_ancestor.go:82-85) deliberately wraps an ancestry-enumeration failure (gitx.Log error after a commit-itself miss) in forge.ErrNoBundle. In the no---or-regen path this is disclosed, but under --or-regen runSync's `errors.Is(err, forge.ErrNoBundle) \u0026\u0026 orRegen` branch (cmd/verdi/sync.go:228) matches it and silently regenerates locally without printing the wrapped error — so a history that could not be walked at all is treated as evidence of absence, and a bundle that may exist at a real ancestor is bypassed with no disclosure that the walk never ran. dc-1's posture is that only a genuine no-bundle result is a fallback signal and non-absence failures surface through the operational path; an unenumerable history is closer to the latter. Practical reachability is low (resolveRefCommit already ran git rev-parse in the same root), which tempers severity.\",\"confidence\":0.35},{\"id\":\"shallow-clone-silently-bounds-unbounded-walk\",\"text\":\"ac-2/dc-1 promise the walk is unbounded — 'continues to the ref's root' — and the exhausted refusal names 'the ref's entire history'. In a shallow or partial clone (fetch-depth:1 is the common CI checkout), `git log \u003ccommit\u003e` (gitx.Log, sync_ancestor.go:34) silently stops at the shallow boundary, so the refusal at sync_ancestor.go:109 claims 'anywhere in N commit(s) walked (oldest..commit)' when only the truncated local graph was walked, and a bundle at a deeper true ancestor is refused with no disclosure that the history itself was incomplete. The fold's gitx.IsAncestor shares the same local-graph blindness, so the two surfaces cannot disagree (the dc-1 authority clause holds), but the spec's 'exhausting the ref's entire history' is silently narrowed to 'exhausting the clone's available history' with no shallow-repo detection or disclosure.\",\"confidence\":0.25},{\"id\":\"env-wins-register-shortfall\",\"text\":\"AC-1's behavioral register asks for an integration test 'proving the explicit CI env still wins byte-identically when both it and a resolvable origin are present' using 'fixturegit plus internal/forge/fake'. The delivered TestGithubIdentifier_CIEnvWinsOverResolvableOrigin (cmd/verdi/sync_test.go:946) proves env-wins at the githubOwnerRepo seam with a real fixturegit remote read via gitx.RemoteURL, but never drives runSync/cmdSync and uses no fake forge — no test observes a sync run end-to-end addressing the forge with the env-resolved identifier while a conflicting origin is configured. This is a defensible register substitution (identifier resolution structurally happens in cmdSync before deps exist, and cmdSync cannot take an injected forge), but it is a quieter register than the spec's text names, undisclosed.\",\"confidence\":0.2}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/sync-local-flow@3d54116abd90b77f3bdc84949689746a4a28f41c, spec/sync-local-flow@a56f64ccd508c9744e7f99e9416d51cf171a3460], digest: sha256:a52cc580f67831100b5d7a8195c059036367b4eb4f7c6d45c4ab2e7e4851f8cd, integrity: sha256:c1abe32fa7e02935797c394473a2829b1a6f8ac8b2987bf5c34aa259deca6c1a }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-or-regen-masks-unwalkable-history** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: fetchAncestorBundle (cmd/verdi/sync_ancestor.go:82-85) deliberately wraps an ancestry-enumeration failure (gitx.Log error after a commit-itself miss) in forge.ErrNoBundle. In the no---or-regen path this is disclosed, but under --or-regen runSync's `errors.Is(err, forge.ErrNoBundle) && orRegen` branch (cmd/verdi/sync.go:228) matches it and silently regenerates locally without printing the wrapped error — so a history that could not be walked at all is treated as evidence of absence, and a bundle that may exist at a real ancestor is bypassed with no disclosure that the walk never ran. dc-1's posture is that only a genuine no-bundle result is a fallback signal and non-absence failures surface through the operational path; an unenumerable history is closer to the latter. Practical reachability is low (resolveRefCommit already ran git rev-parse in the same root), which tempers severity. (confidence 0.35)
- **judged-shallow-clone-silently-bounds-unbounded-walk** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-2/dc-1 promise the walk is unbounded — 'continues to the ref's root' — and the exhausted refusal names 'the ref's entire history'. In a shallow or partial clone (fetch-depth:1 is the common CI checkout), `git log <commit>` (gitx.Log, sync_ancestor.go:34) silently stops at the shallow boundary, so the refusal at sync_ancestor.go:109 claims 'anywhere in N commit(s) walked (oldest..commit)' when only the truncated local graph was walked, and a bundle at a deeper true ancestor is refused with no disclosure that the history itself was incomplete. The fold's gitx.IsAncestor shares the same local-graph blindness, so the two surfaces cannot disagree (the dc-1 authority clause holds), but the spec's 'exhausting the ref's entire history' is silently narrowed to 'exhausting the clone's available history' with no shallow-repo detection or disclosure. (confidence 0.25)
- **judged-env-wins-register-shortfall** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: AC-1's behavioral register asks for an integration test 'proving the explicit CI env still wins byte-identically when both it and a resolvable origin are present' using 'fixturegit plus internal/forge/fake'. The delivered TestGithubIdentifier_CIEnvWinsOverResolvableOrigin (cmd/verdi/sync_test.go:946) proves env-wins at the githubOwnerRepo seam with a real fixturegit remote read via gitx.RemoteURL, but never drives runSync/cmdSync and uses no fake forge — no test observes a sync run end-to-end addressing the forge with the env-resolved identifier while a conflicting origin is configured. This is a defensible register substitution (identifier resolution structurally happens in cmdSync before deps exist, and cmdSync cannot take an injected forge), but it is a quieter register than the spec's text names, undisclosed. (confidence 0.20)
